data.body:
## Completion Verification — FAIL

Verified against every AC in the ticket. Most of the core work is implemented (registry, orchestrator structure, DET floor, facet chunking, applies_at routing, CODEBASE_GROUNDED enforcement, finding-id minting, Pass-4 coach, ISF summary fallback, unit tests). Two requirements are NOT MET:

### NOT MET — AC4/AC6: Runtime context-window escalation ladder

The ticket requires: "batch criteria → ONE criterion/call → escalate to a higher-context model → (still too big) a FAILURE FINDING". `MODEL_LADDER` is defined in `orchestrator.py` (lines 45–49) and `largest_window_tokens()` uses it for P8 budget math. However, there is no runtime escalation code: no logic catches a context-limit error mid-run and retries with smaller chunks or a different model. The only active ladder use is P8's pre-check (which blocks when the ticket is already too big). The "batch → one-per-call → escalate model" runtime path is absent.

### NOT MET — AC5: Container criteria one-child-at-a-time orchestration + absence verification

The ticket requires: "Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time (both whole) and aggregate; absence findings from the per-child view are verified against the COMPLETE set before they stand." G3/G4 are dispatched as regular AGENT-tier criteria through `_run_passes` / `pass1_chunk(agentic=True)` — the orchestrator fires each as a single agent call with the full plan. There is no orchestrator-level loop that iterates children one-at-a-time, accumulates per-child results, and then verifies absence findings against the complete sibling set. The per-child iteration is delegated entirely to the LLM agent's tool use, which does not meet the explicit requirement for deterministic per-child aggregation at the orchestrator level.

### MET
- Registry loads from `criteria_v8.json` via importlib.resources; coverage guard implemented and unit-tested (`test_registry_coverage_guard_passes`).
- Orchestrator filters via `applies_at`, packs by facet (`chunk_by_facet`), dispatches by exec tier (single/2-step/agent/DET).
- `CODEBASE_GROUNDED = frozenset({"E4", "G1G2", "A1", "G6"})` enforced — only these run as agentic (tool-using).
- Full plan content is never chunked; `plan_text` is always passed whole; P8 provides the "too big" failure finding.
- ISF on an oversized session log falls back to a documented summary with reduced-confidence tagging (`_reduced_confidence`, `summarized` flag in coverage).
- DET tier (P1–P8) has an explicit CODE execution path via `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` dispatched before LLM tiers — satisfies the T8 AC.
- Finding ids minted exclusively by `mint_finding_id()` in the orchestrator (content fingerprint, SHA256).
- Pass-4 affirmative coach dispatched after Pass-3 with `MOVE_REGISTRY` (single-turn, no tooling).
- Coverage recorded including DET results, routing, ISF, chunks, counts.
